Reporting of Adverse Events 
Adverse events might occur after administration of an RSV antibody. Reporting of any clinically significant adverse event is encouraged, regardless of whether a causal relationship with the product is certain.
Adverse events after administration of an RSV antibody alone should be reported to FDA’s MedWatch system online ( The FDA Safety Information and Adverse Event Reporting Program | Medwatch ), by fax, by mail, or by calling 800-332-1088. ††††† 
Adverse events after coadministration of an RSV antibody with a vaccine should be reported to the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS) online at Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS) , by fax, by mail, or by calling 800-822-7967. The report should specify that the patient received nirsevimab or clesrovimab. §§§§§ If an adverse event occurs after coadministration of an RSV antibody with a vaccine and is reported to VAERS, a duplicate report to MedWatch is not necessary.
